codeReview.summary: Fail. This rerun replaces launch-comment inference with invocation-trace assertions, but it still does not satisfy the full feature contract: rendered-indicator tests remain renderability-only and cross-surface switch-sync proofs remain store-driven instead of UI-action-driven.

issues.description: `test_ui_crossSurface_dashboardRendersCreatedProfile_withActiveIndicator` still uses renderability-only assertions (`XCTAssertNotNil` at lines 2430 and 2434) instead of concrete rendered indicator seams (`testActiveProfileDisplayName` / `testActiveProfileAccessibilityLabel`) required by expectedBehavior.

`test_ui_crossSurface_popoverRendersCreatedProfile_withActiveIndicator` still uses renderability-only assertions (`XCTAssertNotNil` at lines 2475 and 2479) rather than explicit rendered label/accessibility assertions.

Cross-surface switch synchronization proofs remain store-driven (`localStore.setActiveProfile` at lines 2531 and 2597) instead of using UI callback paths (`testTriggerSelectAndSwitch` / `testTriggerSwitchViaUIAction`) as required.

sharedStateObservations.observation: `switcher-ui-worker` requires manual UI sanity checks, but this rerun handoff reports `followedProcedure=true` with no interactive evidence.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/switcher-ui-worker/SKILL.md step 7 requires manual UI checks; handoff `2026-04-10T13-42-24-814Z__integration-hardening-finalize-crossflow-rendered-ui-launch-trace-evidence__c2b2bdab-d34c-4eda-a7bd-7383e67ee828.json` has `verification.interactiveChecks: []` and `skillFeedback.followedProcedure: true`.

sharedStateObservations.observation: There is recurring confusion about using SwiftUI test seams that depend on `@State` outside a hosted render context; this behavior is not captured in shared testing guidance.

summary: Reviewed feature metadata, rerun handoff/session transcript skeleton, prior failure synthesis, skill requirements, and commit `01921b8cc62428f51cc6819bb7579d5db2ca4a90`. Prior launch-trace failure is addressed, but two original blocking areas remain unresolved: concrete rendered-indicator assertions and UI-action-driven cross-surface switch proofs.
